key_insight: CatRetriever learns a shared latent space for slab and bulk crystals so a slab-generated catalyst can be matched to plausible parent bulk structures.
engineering_relevance: This enables generative catalyst discovery to move beyond surface-only candidates and screen for bulk-viable, synthesizable materials with target adsorption behavior.
method: Contrastive representation learning aligns slab and bulk embeddings.
Slab queries retrieve candidate parent bulks from a database.
An extended pipeline combines retrieval, generative search expansion, and adsorption-energy distribution analysis.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong retrieval performance, with R@1 above 91% and R@3 above 98% on both in-distribution and holdout evaluation sets.
watch_item: Retrieval plausibility does not guarantee true experimental synthesizability or full thermodynamic stability, so downstream validation is still needed.
paper_title: CatRetriever: Contrastive Representation Learning for Slab-to-Bulk Retrieval in Generative Catalyst Discovery
